ground_truth.title: Cheapest Project Management Software: Finding the Best Value for Money
ground_truth.author: Mehak Siddiqui
ground_truth.main_content:
If you want to organize your work on a budget, we’ve found the best-value and cheapest project management software that can help. Discover powerful features for different kinds of workflows and teams to boost productivity while saving money.

Key Takeaways: These Are the Best Cheap Project Management Tools
1. nTask — Cheapest project management software with a great set of features. Get started with the Premium plan for just $3 per user per month (billed annually).
2. ClickUp — Highly customizable project management platform with a 30-day money-back guarantee on paid plans. Start with the free forever plan or upgrade to the Unlimited plan for the best-value option.
3. Trello — An easy-to-use tool focused on kanban boards that works well for simple project management. Start with the free plan and upgrade to Standard for the best value.
4. monday.com — A highly visual, user-friendly and versatile project management platform; the Standard plan offers the best value for money.
5. Asana — An adaptable tool suitable for personal projects or complex resource management. Get a 14-day free trial of the Advanced plan to experience its powerful features.

Facts & Expert Analysis About Affordable Project Management Software
- nTask offers built-in issue and risk tracking: These features are typically included on higher-tier plans only, but nTask makes them available on its entry-level free plan.
- ClickUp features more than 15 project views: Choose between list, board, calendar, Gantt chart or mind map views, with some limited usage available even on the free plan.
- monday.com has more than 245,000 customers globally: Major brands like Coca-Cola, McDonald’s and Hulu are part of its customer base, which highlights its reliability and scalability.

You don’t need to spend a ton of money to organize your projects and boost productivity. We’ve found the cheapest project management software that provide the best value, with robust features to track multiple tasks and help teams of all sizes collaborate. All of these tools also feature on our list of the best project management software.

In this article, we compare the top budget-friendly project management software options to help you find one that best fits your needs. We assess each tool’s features, ease of use and overall value to ensure you’re getting your money’s worth.

Cloudwards’ Choice: nTask
I believe nTask is the best affordable project management software because its entry-level Premium plan (starting at $3 per user per month, annually) includes powerful features like custom fields, Gantt charts, bulk actions and task dependencies. These are usually reserved for more expensive or higher-tier plans on other project management software.
Moreover, even the free version includes native time-tracking and issue-tracking tools, plus core task management and team collaboration features. It’s also very user-friendly, which makes it a great choice for almost any team.

How to Choose the Best Cheap Project Management Software
This roundup focuses on the best project management software apps that offer great value for money. In addition to pricing, we considered key functionalities that help different kinds of teams meet their objectives. Below is a breakdown of what we focused on during our testing and selection process:
- Core task management features: These form the backbone of project management software and include task creation, progress tracking and views like kanban boards and timelines.
- Collaboration tools: Built-in communication features like comments, file sharing, team chat and @mentions help reduce email clutter and keep everyone aligned in real time.
- Automations and integrations: Rule-based automations for repetitive tasks can save time, especially for bigger teams. Integrations ensure seamless data flow between apps like Slack, Google Drive or Zoom to streamline productivity.
- Ease of use: A user-friendly interface helps teams quickly get started with and focus on their projects rather than spend too much time learning or setting up the tool.
- Security and permissions: Options like two-factor authentication and data encryption help protect sensitive project data. At the same time, role-based access provides control over who can access information.

The Top 5 Cheap Project Management Tools Compared
Service: | Free Plan | Starting Price per User | Core Task Management | Collaboration Tools | Automations & Integrations | Ease of Use | Security & Permissions
nTask | ✓ 5 team member limit & 100MB storage; no advanced features like Gantt chart & kanban views | Premium: $3 per month (one-year plan) | Task lists, kanban boards, Gantt charts, time tracking | Meeting scheduling, comments, file sharing, issue tracking | Native integrations like Zoom & Slack, plus Zapier | Simple, intuitive interface | Role-based access
ClickUp | ✓ 60MB storage & limited use of features like Gantt charts & dashboards | Unlimited: $7 per month (one-year plan) | Hierarchical tasks, docs, goals, dashboards, time tracking | Real-time chat, comments, whiteboards, email within tasks | Extensive native integrations & custom automations | Steep learning curve due to many customizable features | Granular permissions, 2FA
Trello | ✓ 10 user limit & 10 boards per workspace | Standard: $5 per month (one-year plan) | Visual kanban boards, checklists, simple tasks | Comments, mentions, file sharing via cards | Power-ups for integrations & basic automations | Very beginner-friendly, drag-and-drop UI | Basic access control, limited enterprise-grade features
monday.com | ✓ 2 user limit, 3 boards & 500MB storage | Standard: $12 per month (one-year plan) | Task tracking, multiple project views, custom workflows | File sharing, team updates, mentions | Easy automation & more than 200 integrations with CRM, marketing & dev tools | Intuitive & visually appealing | Role-based permissions, audit logs, enterprise-level security
Asana | ✓ 10 user limit, no advanced features like dashboard, Gantt chart & timeline views | Starter: $10.99 per month (one-year plan) | Task lists, boards, calendar view, goals, dependencies | Comments, team inbox, project conversations | More than 200 integrations & rule-based automation | Polished, color-coded interface | Role-based permissions, admin controls

1. nTask— Best Overall Cheap Project Management Software
nTask is our top choice because it offers essential task management and collaboration features on a budget. Even the free version of nTask offers native time-tracking and issue management tools, which are usually available only on paid plans. However, it lacks built-in automations, instead relying on integrations with other apps.
nTask Key Features: Available on all plans, including the free one.
- List, task and calendar views: Visualize your workflow in a way that best suits your team for better clarity and planning.
- Comments, file sharing and mentions: Facilitate easy communication with team members to reduce the need for back-and-forth emails.
- Time tracking: Monitor how long tasks take to improve productivity and simplify billing or reporting.
- Issue tracking: Identify, assign and resolve bugs or blockers quickly to keep projects moving forward.
nTask Integrations & Collaboration Capabilities:
- Automation: Zapier
- Collaboration and storage: No native integrations
- Communication: Slack, Zoom, Microsoft Teams
- CRM and marketing: No native integrations
- Software development: No native integrations
- Productivity and team management: Google Calendar, Outlook, Apple Calendar
nTask Hands-On Testing
We appreciate that nTask is an intuitive and easy-to-use project management tool. The interface is clean, with a minimalist dashboard that keeps tasks, meetings and timesheets within easy reach. You can easily switch between views like list, grid and calendar, and the task cards offer ample space for details without being overwhelming.
Creating an account and getting started was a quick and straightforward process. You can immediately begin adding tasks and organizing your workflow. Everything worked as expected, and we didn’t encounter any issues with the web version or the desktop and mobile apps. Overall, nTask offers a solid balance between simplicity and functionality.
nTask Pricing & Value
nTask offers a free Basic plan that’s ideal for small teams of up to five users. However, its Premium plan, starting at just $3 per user per month, is the best deal — it includes features like Gantt charts, kanban boards, bulk actions, subtasks, task dependencies and budget tracking.

2. ClickUp— Best Affordable Project Management Software for Customization
ClickUp includes a wide array of features on its budget plan, including customizable views, real-time collaboration and limited use of automations and dashboards. It also has a generous free plan that supports unlimited users. However, its expansive feature set can feel complex, so it involves a bit of a learning curve to master.
ClickUp Key Features: Available on all plans, including the free one, but some features come with a limited number of uses.
- Multiple project views: Switch between views like list, board, Gantt chart, calendar and timeline to visualize projects the way that best fits your team’s workflow.
- Real-time collaboration: Add comments, tag teammates and edit tasks or docs together in real time.
- Automations: Automate repetitive actions like task assignment, status changes and reminders to ease even the most complex project management.
- Customized dashboards: Get detailed insights into your team’s workloads and project progress to achieve goals on time.
ClickUp Integrations & Collaboration Capabilities:
- Automation: Zapier, Make
- Collaboration and storage: Google Workspace, OneDrive
- Communication: Slack, Zoom
- CRM and marketing: HubSpot, Intercom
- Software development: GitHub, Sentry
- Productivity & team management: Google Calendar, Calendly, TimeCamp
ClickUp Hands-On Testing
ClickUp is packed with features that make it highly customizable for any workflow. The interface is modern and offers granular control over how the software works for you. While this is useful, it can get overwhelming for first-time users, as there’s quite a lot to set up. We therefore appreciate that ClickUp has many videos and tutorials to help you along the way.
The software worked well during our testing, and performance was snappy across its web, desktop and mobile apps. However, it definitely has a steeper learning curve than other software. It’s not as intuitive to navigate, and notifications can pile up quickly if not configured carefully.
ClickUp Pricing & Value
ClickUp offers one of the strongest free plans in the project and resource management space, with unlimited users and tasks, plus 60MB of storage. Meanwhile, the Unlimited plan is the best value at just $7 per user per month (billed annually), — it unlocks unlimited storage and advanced options like resource management.

3. Trello — Budget-Friendly Simple Project Management Software
Trello is a great kanban board tool for project managers who value simplicity. It has features like unlimited workspaces (each with up to 10 boards), task cards and storage, even on its free plan. Additional views like calendar, timeline and dashboard are available on its Premium plan. Unfortunately, Trello lacks advanced automation and reporting features.
Trello Key Features: Available on all plans, including the free one.
- Kanban boards: Assign tasks and organize them into categories like “to do,” “doing” and “done” for a visual overview of your work.
- Collaboration features: Add comments and attach files within tasks to keep all contextual information in one place.
- Templates: Use pre-built board templates to jumpstart common workflows across different needs, like HR, sales and customer relationship management.
- Power-ups: Add extra functionality like calendars, time tracking and third-party integrations to extend Trello’s capabilities beyond basic task management.
- File previews: Preview over 30 file types directly within cards, eliminating the need to download files to view their contents.
- Butler AI: Suggests and creates automated workflow based on your team’s activities, learning from patterns to recommend time-saving automations.
Trello Integrations & Collaboration Capabilities:
- Automation: Zapier, Automate.io, IFTTT, Make, Integrately
- Collaboration and storage: Dropbox, Google Drive, Pipedrive
- Communication: Microsoft Teams, Slack
- CRM and marketing: Salesforce, Mailchimp, Zendesk
- Software development: GitHub, Jira Cloud
- Productivity and team management: Google Calendar, Clockify
Trello Hands-On Testing
Trello is one of the most user-friendly tools for project managers who use kanban boards to organize work. The drag-and-drop functionality felt intuitive during our tests, and the ability to customize cards with checklists, due dates, attachments and labels makes it both simple and surprisingly versatile.
That said, Trello does feel limited when it comes to complex workflows. It lacks the granular customization and advanced features that tools like ClickUp and monday.com have. Features like different views and dashboards are available only on paid plans. Everything works smoothly, but it can be tricky to manage multiple boards as your projects expand.
Trello Pricing & Value
Trello offers a generous free plan that supports 10 team members and unlimited tasks. However, the best deal is the Standard plan, at $5 per user per month. The Standard plan unlocks features such as custom fields and unlimited boards, which are useful for larger projects or teams.

4. monday.com — User-Friendly Project Management Software
monday.com is an easy-to-use project management tool with a comprehensive feature set that’s adaptable and scalable for different team needs. We like that it offers multiple project views, strong collaboration features and built-in automations. However, these are rather limited on the free and Basic plan, and the higher-tier plans can get expensive for larger teams.
monday.com Key Features: Access restricted on some plans.
- Visual workflows: Create customized boards using columns, rows and color-coded statuses to track your tasks visually.
- Team collaboration: Add comments, tag teammates and attach files to communicate directly within tasks.
- Templates: Kickstart your work with more than 200 ready-made templates for different functions, like HR, marketing and design.
- Automations: Set up rule-based automations for repetitive tasks like status updates or notifications (on the Standard plan and higher).
monday.com Integrations & Collaboration Capabilities:
- Automation: Google Drive automations, AI Copilot
- Collaboration and storage: Miro, Google Workspace, OneDrive
- Communication: Loom, Slack, Zoom
- CRM and marketing: Mailchimp, Calendly
- Software development: Go Roadmap, GitHub
- Productivity and team management: Outlook Calendar, Timely
monday.com Hands-On Testing
Using monday.com feels smooth and intuitive right from the start. We like the color-coded interface that makes it easy to understand, even for new users. Boards are organized like dynamic spreadsheets that you can customize according to your project’s needs. Everything feels fluid and responsive, making this a great tool for any kind of user.
That said, the monday.com free and Basic plans are very limited. Automations, integrations and advanced project views like timeline and Gantt chart are available only on higher-tier plans. This makes monday.com a smart choice for organizations that are able to spend a bit more to fully take advantage of the features.
monday.com Pricing & Value
monday.com offers a limited free plan with support for just two members. The Standard plan offers the best value because it includes features like timeline, Gantt and calendar views, guest access, and automations that save time. However, this is the most pricey option on this list, at $12 per user per month, annually.

5. Asana— Cheap & Easy Project Management Software
Asana caters to the needs of cross-functional teams with powerful collaboration features for Agile project management. Marketing teams can use it to plan campaigns and content production, while product teams can manage sprints and feature releases. We consider it the best free project management software for individuals and teams.
Asana Key Features: Some features restricted to certain plans.
- Task management: Create tasks and set due dates using list, board and calendar views.
- Project collaboration: Add comments, attach files and get real-time updates about any changes made.
- Automations: Create unlimited automations (on the Standard plan and higher) to save time and reduce manual effort.
- Project templates: Choose from built-in, customizable templates for common workflows such as sales, design and HR (on the Standard plan and higher).
Asana Integrations & Collaboration Capabilities:
- Automation: Splunk, Zapier
- Collaboration and storage: Dropbox, OneDrive, Google Drive
- Communication: Microsoft Teams, Slack, Gmail
- CRM and marketing: Salesforce, HubSpot, Freshdesk
- Software development: Azure DevOps, BetterBugs
- Productivity and team management: InstaGantt, Tick, Google Docs
Asana Hands-On Testing
Using Asana is straightforward and intuitive thanks to its color-coded interface. It’s easy to get a quick overview of tasks, deadlines and project progress from the home screen. We like that it’s flexible enough to adapt to different projects and work styles. The onboarding tutorials and tool tips also make it friendly for beginners.
However, Asana has a few quirks that may frustrate some users. Its free plan lacks some key tools, like a timeline view and Gantt charts, which are available only on paid plans. The abundance of features and options can also feel overwhelming at first, particularly for first-time users.
Asana Pricing & Value
Asana’s free version is great for up to 10 users, but the best deal is the Starter plan, starting at $10.99 per user per month. It includes advanced features like custom fields, Gantt charts, unlimited automations and dashboards, which are essential for managing complex projects.

Honorable Mentions: Cheap Project Management Software
- Zoho Projects: A budget-friendly option for teams already using Zoho’s ecosystem. It offers a free plan for up to three users and paid plans starting at $5 per user per month.
- Wrike: A powerful tool for larger teams with complex workflows. Its free plan supports unlimited users but has limited features, while paid plans start at $9.80 per user per month.
- Teamwork: A great option for client-facing teams, with a free plan for up to five users and paid plans starting at $10 per user per month.

Our Methodology: How We Tested the Most Affordable Project Management Tools
We evaluated each software based on the following criteria:
1. Pricing and value: We looked at the cost of entry-level plans and what features are included at each price point.
2. Core features: We assessed task management, collaboration tools, project views and reporting capabilities.
3. Ease of use: We considered the learning curve, interface design and onboarding process.
4. Integrations: We examined the availability of native integrations and third-party app connections.
5. Scalability: We evaluated how well each tool grows with your team and projects.

Free vs Paid Project Management Software
Free plans are great for small teams or individuals just getting started with project management. They typically include basic task management features but limit advanced functionality, storage and the number of users.
Paid plans unlock more powerful features like:
- Advanced project views (Gantt charts, timelines)
- Automation capabilities
- Increased storage limits
- More integrations
- Better security and admin controls
- Priority support
For most teams, the best value comes from entry-level paid plans that balance cost with essential features.

Final Thoughts
Choosing the right cheap project management software depends on your team’s specific needs, budget and workflow preferences. Here’s a quick guide:
- Choose nTask if you want the most affordable option with good basic features.
- Choose ClickUp if you need maximum customization and don’t mind a learning curve.
- Choose Trello if you prefer simple kanban boards and visual task management.
- Choose monday.com if you want a user-friendly interface with strong collaboration features.
- Choose Asana if you need a versatile tool for cross-functional teams.
All of these tools offer free trials or free plans, so you can test them before committing. Remember that the cheapest option isn’t always the best value—consider what features you actually need to manage your projects effectively.

FAQ: Cheap Project Management Apps
What is the cheapest project management software?
nTask offers the lowest starting price at $3 per user per month for its Premium plan when billed annually.
Is there any completely free project management software?
Yes, all the tools on our list offer free plans with varying limitations. Trello and ClickUp have particularly generous free plans.
What features should I look for in cheap project management software?
Look for core task management, collaboration tools, multiple project views, and essential integrations. Consider your team size and workflow complexity.
Can I use free project management software for business?
Yes, but free plans often have limitations on users, storage and features. For growing businesses, paid plans usually offer better value.
How do I choose between these options?
Consider your team size, budget, required features and preferred workflow style. Most tools offer free trials so you can test them before deciding.
with: nTask is our top choice because it offers essential task management and collaboration features on a budget.
ClickUp includes a wide array of features on its budget plan, including customizable views, real-time collaboration and limited use of automations and dashboards.
Trello is a great kanban board tool for project managers who value simplicity.
